string.text:
The Hungarian M35 bolt-action rifle was a locally produced variant of the Mannlicher M95, chambered in 7.62x54r and used primarily by Hungary during the interwar period and World War II. It featured the same straight-pull bolt system for rapid cycling, a five-round en-bloc clip, and a sturdy design suited for military use. The M35 was an improvement over earlier models, often featuring better metallurgy and sights. While largely replaced by semi-automatic rifles later in the war, it remained in use with Hungarian and Axis-aligned forces.
 
 %c[ui_gray_2]ATTACHMENTS: 
 %c[d_purple] - %c[ui_gray_2] Scopes: ZF-4 Scope.
